rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared helper variables with matching types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-specific subcomponent ports directly.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq declarations for helper variables in AGREE contracts.
Place component type annexes in component type scopes, implementation annexes in implementation scopes.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference valid AADL feature names and types.
Do not mix AADL property names directly into AGREE expressions without explicit model exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type contracts.
Do not assume Boolean ports can be treated as integer values in range checks.
Do not reference AADL timing properties like Period or Deadline directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not generate range assumptions without visible feature names or concrete bounds.
